critic_round1_raw:
BiLSTM vs XGBoost for card fraud detection.

1. 90-DAY INVESTIGATION WINDOW AND RECENT EXCLUSION: Transactions in the final 90 days (Oct-Dec 2023) are excluded to ensure label completeness. This means the test set effectively ends in June 2023 (months 31-33 of 36). Wait - the test split is months 31-36 (Jul-Dec 2023), but the final 90 days are excluded for label completeness. Actually, re-reading: "Transactions in the final 90 days are excluded to ensure label completeness" refers to the full dataset's final 90 days, meaning the dataset through December 2023 has labels complete for transactions through approximately September 2023. The test set (months 31-36, Jul-Dec 2023) is partially affected by this exclusion.

But actually, the design states the 90-day investigation window is for LABEL ASSIGNMENT, not temporal split. The exclusion prevents test set contamination from labels not yet finalized. This is correct practice.

2. THRESHOLD CALIBRATION AT 1% FPR: The 1% FPR threshold is calibrated on the validation set and applied to both models on the test set. This is correct - the threshold is defined pre-test.
defender_round1_raw:
On label completeness: the 90-day exclusion window ensures all transactions evaluated in the test set have complete fraud investigation outcomes. The test set (months 31-36) minus final 90 days means test transactions from July through approximately September 2023 have complete labels. This is the standard approach for fraud model evaluation where investigation takes weeks to complete.

On threshold calibration: the 1% FPR threshold calibrated on the validation set and applied identically to both models on the test set is correct. No look-ahead on the test set threshold selection.
critic_round2_raw: Both points confirmed. Label exclusion is standard fraud evaluation practice. Threshold calibration on validation is correct. No remaining concern.
adjudication_raw: Run 1: All concerns resolved. Verdict: defense_wins.
